# Requirements Quality Checklist: Heist Detail Page

**Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, consistency, and measurability
of the requirements in `spec.md` — as it stands after FR-013, FR-014, the
FR-007 correction, the Non-Goals section, and both Clarifications sessions
(2026-07-06, 2026-07-09) — before proceeding to a fresh `/speckit-plan` pass.
**Created**: 2026-07-09
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command.
It tests whether the requirements are well-written — not whether the
implementation behaves correctly.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Is the visual treatment that makes status "not text-only" (FR-003) specified within the requirement itself, rather than only implied by an Assumption about reusing the Badge component? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK002 Are accessibility requirements defined for the manual "Refresh" action (FR-014) with the same rigor FR-012 gives to "back to list" and "retry"? [Gap, Spec §FR-012, FR-014]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the expected UI state while a refresh (FR-014) or retry (FR-011) is in flight — e.g., does existing content stay visible, does a loading indicator reappear, is the button disabled — specified anywhere? [Gap, Spec §FR-009, FR-011, FR-014]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for what displays when `createdByCodename` or `assignedToCodename` is empty/missing, mirroring the empty-description (FR-002) and missing-deadline (FR-013) placeholder pattern? [Gap, Spec §FR-004, FR-005]
- [ ] CHK005 Is there a requirement (not just an Assumption) establishing that date/time values render in the user's local browser timezone? [Gap, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK006 Are concrete breakpoint/viewport requirements defined for the "mobile-responsive... usable on small screens" Assumption, or does it remain unquantified? [Gap, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "human-readable date and time format" (FR-006, FR-007) defined with a concrete example or format spec, or left to implementer interpretation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, FR-007]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "visually and textually different from the not-found state" (FR-011) quantified with specific distinguishing criteria (e.g., icon, color, copy), or left open to interpretation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "meaningful loading indicator" (User Story 2 narrative, FR-009) defined with any concrete form (spinner, skeleton, text), or is the term left ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "raw error text, stack traces, or Firestore error codes" (FR-011) precise enough to unambiguously classify all possible Firestore SDK error shapes, or could edge-case error objects fall outside this definition? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do FR-011's "retry" action and FR-014's "Refresh" action name the same or a different operation? If different, is the distinction (when each appears, what each does) stated anywhere in the spec? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, FR-014]
- [ ] CHK012 Does FR-006 cross-reference FR-013's "No deadline set" placeholder, or could a reader of FR-006 alone assume the deadline is always present? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK013 Is SC-004's 100ms loading-indicator threshold reflected back in FR-009, or does the measurable outcome exist only in Success Criteria without a corresponding functional requirement? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, SC-004]
- [ ] CHK014 Does the Key Entities description of `deadline` ("may be undefined on legacy documents") stay consistent with FR-006's unconditional "MUST display... deadline," or does FR-006 need the same undefined-case caveat FR-013 already carries? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Key Entities]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK015 Is SC-001's "without requiring any additional interaction or scrolling" measurable only for the stated 1280×800 desktop viewport, with no equivalent measurable outcome for the mobile-responsive requirement in Assumptions? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK016 Can SC-002's "visually distinguishable... without relying solely on text" be objectively verified, or does it depend on the same unquantified treatment flagged in CHK001? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, FR-003]
- [ ] CHK017 Is there a measurable success criterion for the FR-014 Refresh action (e.g., latency, visible feedback), or does it lack an SC counterpart the way FR-009's loading indicator has SC-004? [Gap, Spec §FR-014, Success Criteria]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK018 Does the spec define behavior for a Firestore "permission-denied" response specifically — as distinct from "not found" (FR-010) and generic "network/Firestore error" (FR-011) — given the project's authenticated-read rules? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, FR-011]
- [ ] CHK019 Is behavior specified for triggering Refresh (FR-014) or retry (FR-011) multiple times in rapid succession (e.g., double-click), to prevent overlapping fetches? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-011, FR-014]
- [ ] CHK020 Are requirements defined for the case where a refresh (FR-014) succeeds but returns a "not found" result (heist was deleted after the page loaded), i.e., does the page transition from the loaded state to the not-found state? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-014, FR-010]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Is the interaction between the expired-deadline edge case ("no 'overdue' label required") and the missing-deadline placeholder (FR-013) — i.e., are these two states visually distinguishable from each other — addressed? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-013]
- [ ] CHK022 Are boundary requirements defined for extremely long `title`, `description`, or codename values and their effect on the "no scrolling" success criterion (SC-001)? [Gap, Edge Case, Spec §SC-001]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK023 Does the spec state any performance/latency expectation for the initial `getDoc` fetch itself, beyond the 100ms loading-indicator-appearance threshold in SC-004 (which measures indicator display, not fetch completion)? [Gap, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK024 Are focus-management requirements specified for when the page transitions into the not-found or error state (FR-012), e.g., does focus move to the status message for screen-reader users? [Gap, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK025 Is the sanitization/trust boundary for user-controlled codename strings resolved by a requirement, or does it remain the explicitly flagged open item noted in Assumptions? [Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK026 Is the Assumption that "the existing Badge component... is reused to maintain visual consistency" validated against FR-003, or could a differently-styled status indicator still satisfy FR-003 as written? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, FR-003]
- [ ] CHK027 Is the dependency on "existing authentication middleware" (Edge Cases) documented with enough detail to know what the detail page must NOT re-implement, or is this left implicit? [Dependency, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK028 Now that Non-Goals explicitly excludes real-time updates and mandates the YAGNI decision against `onSnapshot`, does FR-014's Refresh requirement fully account for that constraint (e.g., no requirement accidentally implies live updates)? [Consistency, Spec §Non-Goals, FR-014]
- [ ] CHK029 Are there any remaining `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` markers in the spec, or have both Clarifications sessions (2026-07-06, 2026-07-09) resolved all previously open questions? [Traceability, Spec §Clarifications]

## Notes

- This is a full regeneration of `requirements.md` against the current
  `spec.md` (post FR-013/FR-014/Non-Goals/two Clarifications sessions) — not
  an append to the prior "Specification Quality Checklist" gate, which
  validated an earlier draft and is superseded by this file.
- Items intentionally avoid re-litigating what the 2026-07-09 Clarifications
  session already closed (legacy `deadline` handling, staleness/refresh) and
  what spec.md's trailing process note already logs as resolved
  (CHK002/CHK003 from the real M4 run).
- Check items off as findings are triaged: `[x]` for resolved, leave `[ ]`
  with an inline note for accepted-as-is or deferred items.
